title: streptanthus glandulosus var. albidus
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus var. albidus, also known as White Sticky-poppy or White Tower-stemmed Poppy, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the mustard family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as potentially supporting immune function and reducing stress. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
